summary: A future empirical research program extending the qualitative capstone into RAG corruption conditions.
final_use_case: It makes the next study more than a generic Poison-RAG test: the real question is which adversarial evidence pressures impose the greatest verification burden, where the human reviewer or retrieval defense spends scarce attention, and when a defensive layer becomes easier to saturate than to defeat directly.

instruments.input: clean documents, poisoned variants, source identity, poison specification, and admission rule
instruments.operation: tests whether contaminated material enters the corpus before indexing and records the attack surface at the corpus boundary
instruments.output: admission decision and attack-surface record as a reviewable artifact
instruments.upstream_dependency: Requires clean documents, poisoned variants, source identity, poison specification, and admission rule
instruments.downstream_use: Feeds admission decision and attack-surface record as a reviewable artifact; later claims should not rely on this step until its expected output is present.
instruments.proof: Expected proof artifact: admission decision and attack-surface record as a reviewable artifact
instruments.status: Status: This page is presented as a research idea rather than as completed work because that is what it is. There is no code yet, no pilot run, no IRB.
instruments.gap: Verify one representative case: the step should accept clean documents, poisoned variants, source identity, poison specification, and admission rule and produce admission decision and attack-surface record as a.
instruments.verification_check: Replay one clean/poisoned case through Clean / Poisoned Evidence Admission; confirm exposure, uptake or rejection, reviewer burden, and output: admission decision and attack-surface record....

instruments.input: low-rate adversarial contamination distributed across apparently ordinary source material
instruments.operation: models the long-game attacker who degrades retrieval trust without triggering obvious anomaly thresholds
instruments.output: slow-contamination exposure profile as a reviewable artifact
instruments.upstream_dependency: Requires low-rate adversarial contamination distributed across apparently ordinary source material
instruments.downstream_use: Feeds slow-contamination exposure profile as a reviewable artifact; later claims should not rely on this step until its expected output is present.
instruments.proof: Expected proof artifact: slow-contamination exposure profile as a reviewable artifact
instruments.status: Status: This page is presented as a research idea rather than as completed work because that is what it is. There is no code yet, no pilot run, no IRB.
instruments.gap: Verify one representative case: the step should accept low-rate adversarial contamination distributed across apparently ordinary source material and produce slow-contamination exposure profile as a reviewable artifact.
instruments.verification_check: Replay one clean/poisoned case through Slow-Poison Track; confirm exposure, uptake or rejection, reviewer burden, and output: slow-contamination exposure profile as a reviewable....

instruments.input: high-salience injected poison, prompt-adjacent poison, or sudden source surge
instruments.operation: models the short-game attacker who tries to force immediate retrieval or generation uptake
instruments.output: fast-injection uptake and detection record as a reviewable artifact
instruments.upstream_dependency: Requires high-salience injected poison, prompt-adjacent poison, or sudden source surge
instruments.downstream_use: Feeds fast-injection uptake and detection record as a reviewable artifact; later claims should not rely on this step until its expected output is present.

instruments.verification_check: Replay one clean/poisoned case through Fast-Injection Track; confirm exposure, uptake or rejection, reviewer burden, and output: fast-injection uptake and detection record....

instruments.input: poison wrapped in credible authority cues plus missing or displaced contrary evidence
instruments.operation: tests whether the system mistakes source costume for authority or misses the significance of absent evidence
instruments.output: authority/omission vulnerability record as a reviewable artifact
instruments.upstream_dependency: Requires poison wrapped in credible authority cues plus missing or displaced contrary evidence
instruments.downstream_use: Feeds authority/omission vulnerability record as a reviewable artifact; later claims should not rely on this step until its expected output is present.

instruments.input: embedding index, sparse index, poisoned chunks, clean chunks, reranking, and context-window assembly
instruments.operation: tracks whether poisoned material is surfaced, ranked, fused, or suppressed at each retrieval stage
instruments.output: retrieval trace and contamination exposure map
instruments.upstream_dependency: Requires embedding index, sparse index, poisoned chunks, clean chunks, reranking, and context-window assembly
instruments.downstream_use: Feeds retrieval trace and contamination exposure map; later claims should not rely on this step until its expected output is present.

instruments.input: retrieved context, model answer, source citations, omitted contrary evidence, and analyst review
instruments.operation: measures whether the answer absorbs, paraphrases, amplifies, hides, or resists the poison
instruments.output: uptake, deformation, omission, and review-burden score
instruments.upstream_dependency: Requires retrieved context, model answer, source citations, omitted contrary evidence, and analyst review

instruments.input: slow poison, fast injection, decoys, saturation material, precision poison, and detector budget
instruments.operation: uses layered air-defense logic to test whether cheap attacks can waste expensive review and detection resources while precision poison slips through
instruments.output: detector/reviewer burden result and resource-allocation warning
instruments.upstream_dependency: Requires slow poison, fast injection, decoys, saturation material, precision poison, and detector budget
instruments.downstream_use: Feeds detector/reviewer burden result and resource-allocation warning; later claims should not rely on this step until its expected output is present.
